summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (Python, Timer trigger) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster and persists the result to an Azure Blob Storage container. Authentication must use Managed Identity exclusively — no API keys. Infrastructure is provisioned via Bicep on Azure (Linux Consumption plan). CI/CD runs via GitHub Actions.
assumptions: Python 3.11 is the target runtime based on repository memory of existing Bicep infra.
TRAPI endpoint and GPT-4o model deployment name will be provided as app settings (not secrets/keys) via managed identity access.
Blob container name is 'yankees-roster' per existing infra memory.
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName) — no storage connection strings.
Bicep modules already exist under /infra and will be extended, not rewritten from scratch.
The nightly schedule is midnight UTC (cron: '0 0 * * *').
A TRAPI-compatible endpoint accessible via Azure Managed Identity is available in the target subscription.
GitHub Actions is the CI/CD platform; a service principal or federated identity credential is available for deployment.
Application Insights will be used for observability.
Unit tests will use pytest with pytest-cov.
open_questions: What is the TRAPI endpoint URL and GPT-4o deployment name to use?
What Azure subscription and resource group should the resources be deployed to?
Should the roster blob be a single overwrite per night or versioned/timestamped per run?
Is there an existing Application Insights instance to reuse, or should a new one be provisioned?
What is the expected output format of the roster stored in blob (JSON array, CSV, plain text)?
Are there specific performance or SLA targets for the nightly run completion time?
What alert channel (email, Teams, PagerDuty) should failure notifications go to?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish approved plan, provision all required Azure infrastructure, and configure identity/RBAC so the Function App can access Storage and TRAPI without API keys.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group are identified
TRAPI endpoint and GPT-4o deployment name are confirmed
exit_criteria: Storage account, blob container, Function App, and Application Insights provisioned via Bicep
All infra PRs merged with @copilot review complete

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement and unit-test the TRAPI client, blob writer, and Timer-triggered Azure Function that orchestrates the nightly roster fetch-and-store workflow.
entry_criteria: Function App is reachable and managed identity is confirmed active
TRAPI endpoint accessible from the Function App's identity
exit_criteria: TRAPI client returns 1985 Yankees roster in expected format
Blob writer stores roster to 'yankees-roster' container
Timer trigger fires on nightly schedule (verified in staging)
All unit tests pass with >= 80% coverage
All Core Build PRs merged with @copilot review complete

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add error handling and retry logic, enforce zero-API-key security posture, and wire structured logging and alerting into Application Insights.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Application Insights resource provisioned and instrumentation key/connection string configured via app settings
exit_criteria: No API keys or secrets present in code or configuration
Retry and poison-message handling verified via unit and integration tests
Structured logs emitted to Application Insights on each run
Alert rule configured for function failures
All Hardening PRs merged with @copilot review complete

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end nightly workflow in a staging environment, confirm CI/CD pipeline, and complete deployment to production.
entry_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow exists and can deploy to staging
All unit and integration tests passing in CI
exit_criteria: End-to-end test confirms roster fetched from GPT-4o and stored in blob nightly
CI/CD pipeline deploys successfully to production
Monitoring and alerting active in production
All success criteria from spec.md verified

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan for completeness, feasibility, and alignment with spec.md requirements. Confirm open questions are resolved and assign owners to Foundation wave tasks before work begins.
outputs: Approved task plan with owners assigned, open questions resolved or triaged
acceptance_criteria: All open questions have been answered or explicitly deferred with a rationale
Task owners assigned for all Foundation wave tasks
Plan reviewed and approved by rciapala (owner) and tech lead
GitHub Issues created for all tasks in this plan
risks: Unresolved TRAPI endpoint or deployment name blocks APP-01
Missing Azure subscription/RG info blocks all INF tasks

tasks.title: Provision Azure Storage Account and yankees-roster blob container via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend or confirm /infra Bicep modules to provision an Azure Storage Account with a blob container named 'yankees-roster'. Use identity-based storage connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName) — no connection strings or keys. Apply tags project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala.
outputs: Storage account and blob container provisioned
acceptance_criteria: Storage account deployed with kind=StorageV2, minimum TLS 1.2, public blob access disabled
Blob container 'yankees-roster' exists in the storage account
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName app setting configured (no storage connection string or key)
Tags project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala applied to resource
Infrastructure validated by running `az storage container show --name yankees-roster`
Bicep changes submitted as PR with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: Existing Bicep modules may conflict if storage account already exists — verify idempotency
Identity-based storage requires correct RBAC before Function App can write blobs

tasks.title: Provision Azure Function App (Python 3.11, Linux Consumption) with system-assigned managed identity
tasks.description: Extend or confirm /infra/modules/functionapp.bicep to deploy a Linux Consumption Function App (Python 3.11) with system-assigned managed identity enabled. Configure TRAPI endpoint URL and GPT-4o deployment name as app settings (not secrets). Provision Application Insights and wire the connection.
outputs: Function App deployed with managed identity active
App settings for TRAPI_ENDPOINT and GPT4O_DEPLOYMENT_NAME configured
acceptance_criteria: Function App deployed with kind=functionapp,linux, reserved=true, linuxFxVersion=Python|3.11
System-assigned managed identity is enabled and principal ID is retrievable
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and GPT4O_DEPLOYMENT_NAME app settings present (no secret values inline)
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting wired to Application Insights resource
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName set to storage account name (identity-based, no key)
Validated via `az functionapp show` confirming identity.type=SystemAssigned
Bicep changes submitted as PR with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: Cold-start latency on Consumption plan may affect nightly run SLA — acceptable for batch workloads
TRAPI endpoint may require additional network or identity configuration not yet known

tasks.title: Configure RBAC roles for Function App managed identity on Storage and TRAPI
tasks.description: Assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' (or Owner) to the Function App's managed identity on the Storage Account so it can write blobs to 'yankees-roster'. Assign any required Cognitive Services or TRAPI-specific roles needed to call GPT-4o without API keys.
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor (or Blob Data Owner) role assigned to managed identity on storage account
Any required Cognitive Services / TRAPI role assigned to managed identity
No storage account keys or TRAPI API keys present anywhere in infra or app config
Validated by running a test blob write from the Function App identity using `az rest` or a smoke test function
Bicep RBAC changes submitted as PR with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: TRAPI may not support managed identity auth — if so, must escalate to resolve before APP-01 can proceed
RBAC propagation delay (up to 10 min) can cause false failures in early testing

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights resource via Bicep
tasks.description: Add or confirm an Application Insights resource in /infra Bicep modules. Wire the connection string to the Function App as APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Apply standard project tags.
outputs: Connection string available as output for INF-02
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights resource deployed and active in Azure portal
Connection string output available from Bicep module
Tags project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala applied
Validated via `az monitor app-insights component show`
Bicep changes submitted as PR with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: If an Application Insights workspace already exists, ensure re-use doesn't break existing dashboards

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client to query GPT-4o for 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python TRAPI client module that authenticates to the GPT-4o endpoint using DefaultAzureCredential (managed identity, no API keys), sends a prompt to retrieve the complete 1985 New York Yankees roster, and returns a structured list of players. Include unit tests using pytest with mocked TRAPI responses.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL (from INF-02 app settings)
GPT-4o deployment name (from INF-02 app settings)
acceptance_criteria: trapi_client.get_1985_yankees_roster() returns a non-empty list of player names/records
Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential — no API keys or connection strings in code
Unit tests in tests/test_trapi_client.py cover: successful response, empty response, HTTP error, auth failure
All tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py -v --cov=trapi_client --cov-report=term-missing`
Test coverage >= 80% for trapi_client module (link test results in PR)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: TRAPI managed identity auth may not be supported — requires early validation (see INF-03)
GPT-4o response format may vary — prompt engineering needed for consistent structured output
Rate limiting or quota on GPT-4o deployment

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer for roster data
tasks.description: Implement a Python blob storage writer module that authenticates using DefaultAzureCredential and writes the roster data (as JSON) to the 'yankees-roster' blob container in Azure Storage. Include unit tests with mocked Azure SDK calls.
acceptance_criteria: blob_writer.write_roster(players) uploads a JSON blob to 'yankees-roster' container using identity-based auth
Blob name follows a deterministic or timestamped naming convention (e.g., roster.json or roster-YYYYMMDD.json)
No storage account keys or connection strings used anywhere in the module
Unit tests in tests/test_blob_writer.py cover: successful upload, storage error, empty roster guard
All tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/test_blob_writer.py -v --cov=blob_writer --cov-report=term-missing`
Test coverage >= 80% for blob_writer module (link test results in PR)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: Identity-based blob auth requires RBAC to propagate before integration tests can run
Overwrite vs. append strategy for nightly runs needs decision (see open questions)

tasks.title: Implement nightly Timer-triggered Azure Function orchestrating roster fetch and store
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function entry point with a Timer trigger configured for nightly execution (cron: '0 0 * * *'). The function calls APP-01 TRAPI client and APP-02 blob writer in sequence, logging start/completion and player count. Include unit tests for the orchestration logic.
acceptance_criteria: Timer trigger configured with schedule '0 0 * * *' (midnight UTC nightly)
Function calls trapi_client.get_1985_yankees_roster() then blob_writer.write_roster()
Function logs run start, player count retrieved, blob write confirmation, and any errors
Unit tests in tests/test_function_app.py cover: successful run, TRAPI failure, blob write failure
All tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/test_function_app.py -v --cov=function_app --cov-report=term-missing`
Test coverage >= 80% for function orchestration module (link test results in PR)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: Timer trigger cold start on Consumption plan may add latency — acceptable for nightly batch
If either TRAPI or blob write fails, partial state must not leave corrupt data in container

tasks.title: Validate zero-API-key security posture and secrets hygiene
tasks.description: Run a security scan across all application code, Bicep, and GitHub Actions workflows to confirm no API keys, connection strings, or plaintext secrets are present. Verify all Azure resource access uses managed identity. Document the identity flow from Function App to Storage and TRAPI.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning enabled on repository with no alerts
No API keys, connection strings, or SAS tokens found in code, config, or workflow files
All Azure SDK calls use DefaultAzureCredential (managed identity in production)
RBAC assignments reviewed: principle of least privilege confirmed for storage and TRAPI roles
Identity flow documented: Function App → Managed Identity → Storage Blob Data Contributor → yankees-roster container
PR (if remediation needed) created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Developer machines may have local .env files with keys — must ensure these are in .gitignore
TRAPI access model may require a workaround if managed identity is unsupported

tasks.title: Implement structured logging, Application Insights telemetry, and failure alerts
tasks.description: Add structured logging (JSON format) to the Function App using the Azure Functions logger. Configure Application Insights to capture function invocation metrics, exceptions, and custom events (run start, player count, blob write). Create an Azure Monitor alert rule for function execution failures.
outputs: Alert rule Bicep or ARM template
acceptance_criteria: Function emits structured log entries for: run_start, players_fetched (with count), blob_written, run_complete, and any errors
Logs visible in Application Insights 'traces' and 'exceptions' tables within 5 minutes of a test run
Custom metric or event 'players_fetched_count' queryable in Application Insights
Azure Monitor alert rule triggers when function execution fails (exit code non-zero or exception logged)
Alert notification channel confirmed (email or webhook — to be determined per open question)
Unit tests confirm logger.info/error called with correct structured fields: `python -m pytest tests/test_logging.py -v`
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: Application Insights SDK overhead is negligible for nightly batch — no performance risk
Alert channel not yet defined — may delay final alert configuration

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for build, test, and deploy
tasks.description: Create or extend GitHub Actions workflow(s) to: run pytest on every PR, validate Bicep templates (bicep build), deploy Bicep infra to staging on merge to main, and deploy the Function App code to staging. Use federated identity credential (OIDC) for Azure authentication — no service principal secrets.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow runs pytest on every PR and fails the PR if any test fails
Bicep template validated with `az bicep build` in CI
CD workflow deploys Bicep infra and Function App code to staging on merge to main
Azure authentication uses OIDC federated credential — no client secrets in GitHub Secrets
Pipeline run link and test results included in PR description
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and PR merged
risks: OIDC federated credential must be configured in Entra ID before pipeline can authenticate
Staging environment must be provisioned before CD pipeline can validate deployments

tasks.title: Execute end-to-end integration test: nightly roster fetch and blob store
tasks.description: Run a full end-to-end integration test in the staging environment by manually triggering the Timer function and verifying that the 1985 Yankees roster is fetched from GPT-4o via TRAPI and stored as a valid JSON blob in the 'yankees-roster' container. Validate player completeness and blob contents.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint accessible from staging managed identity
acceptance_criteria: Timer function manually triggered in staging via Azure portal or `az functionapp function invoke`
Blob 'roster.json' (or equivalent) created/updated in 'yankees-roster' container with non-empty player list
Roster contains at least the known key 1985 Yankees players (e.g., Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, Rickey Henderson)
Application Insights shows successful run with players_fetched_count > 0 and no exceptions
No API keys or secrets used during the run (confirmed via SEC-01 sign-off)
E2E test report linked in PR/issue, Go/No-Go sign-off from rciapala before production deployment
risks: GPT-4o may return an incomplete or inconsistent roster — may require prompt tuning (APP-01 risk)
Staging RBAC misconfiguration could cause false failures — use INF-03 validation as pre-check
